title: Writing - Part 1: Healthy Eating
description: Your class has been discussing ways to promote healthier eating habits among young people.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the measures in your notes. You should explain which measure you think would be most effective, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Nutritious school lunches ensure that young people receive at least one healthy meal a day.
Teaching children to cook from an early age helps them develop good eating habits for life.
Banning adverts for sugary and fatty foods during children's TV programmes would reduce their influence on young audiences.

structure.introduction: Introduce the problem of poor eating habits among young people and the need for action.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first measure and how it could help address unhealthy eating.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second measure and argue which would have a greater long-term impact.
structure.conclusion: Give your final recommendation for promoting healthier eating among young people.

content.modelAnswer:
Poor dietary habits among young people are a growing concern, contributing to rising rates of obesity and related health problems. Two effective measures for addressing this issue are improving school meals and introducing mandatory cooking classes.

Providing nutritious, affordable meals in schools ensures that children have access to healthy food regardless of their family's circumstances. When schools serve well-balanced lunches, they help establish good dietary patterns and demonstrate that healthy food can also be tasty and satisfying. This is particularly important for students from low-income backgrounds who may not have access to nutritious food at home.

Teaching children to cook, however, offers a more lasting solution. When young people learn how to prepare meals from fresh ingredients, they are more likely to continue eating healthily as adults. Cooking skills foster independence, creativity and an appreciation of real food over processed alternatives.

In conclusion, both measures are valuable, but I believe cooking education has the most enduring impact. Skills learned in childhood stay with individuals for life, gradually transforming the eating habits of future generations.
